Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class OrderCreate

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.OrderCreate
public class OrderCreate extends Object
OrderCreate - The message describing an order

  • Constructor Details

    • OrderCreate

      public OrderCreate(AssetType assetType, Optional<? extends BrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity, String clientOrderId, org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime, Optional<? extends CommissionCreate> commission, Optional<String> currencyCode, Optional<? extends List<FeeCreate>> fees, String identifier, Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode, IdentifierType identifierType, Optional<? extends LetterOfIntentCreate> letterOfIntent, Optional<? extends LimitPriceCreate> limitPrice, Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> maxSellQuantity, Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> notionalValue, DateCreate orderDate, OrderType orderType, Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> quantity, Optional<? extends RightsOfAccumulationCreate> rightsOfAccumulation, Side side, Optional<? extends List<SpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions, Optional<? extends StopPriceCreate> stopPrice, TimeInForce timeInForce, Optional<? extends TradingSession> tradingSession)

    • OrderCreate

      public OrderCreate(AssetType assetType, String clientOrderId, String identifier, IdentifierType identifierType, DateCreate orderDate, OrderType orderType, Side side, TimeInForce timeInForce)

  • Method Details

    • assetType

      public AssetType assetType()
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • brokerCapacity

      public Optional<BrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity()
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • clientOrderId

      public String clientOrderId()
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • clientReceivedTime

      public org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime()
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • commission

      public Optional<CommissionCreate> commission()
      A custom commission applied to an order

    • currencyCode

      public Optional<String> currencyCode()
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • fees

      public Optional<List<FeeCreate>> fees()
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • identifier

      public String identifier()
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • identifierIssuingRegionCode

      public Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode()
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • identifierType

      public IdentifierType identifierType()
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • letterOfIntent

      public Optional<LetterOfIntentCreate> letterOfIntent()
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • limitPrice

      public Optional<LimitPriceCreate> limitPrice()
      A limit price definition

    • maxSellQuantity

      public Optional<DecimalCreate> maxSellQuantity()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • notionalValue

      public Optional<DecimalCreate> notionalValue()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • orderDate

      public DateCreate orderDate()
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • orderType

      public OrderType orderType()
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, and LIMIT are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • quantity

      public Optional<DecimalCreate> quantity()
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

    • rightsOfAccumulation

      public Optional<RightsOfAccumulationCreate> rightsOfAccumulation()
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

    • side

      public Side side()
      The side of this order.

    • specialReportingInstructions

      public Optional<List<SpecialReportingInstructions>> specialReportingInstructions()
      Special Reporting Instructions to be applied to this order. Can include multiple Instructions.

    • stopPrice

      public Optional<StopPriceCreate> stopPrice()
      A stop price definition

    • timeInForce

      public TimeInForce timeInForce()
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • tradingSession

      public Optional<TradingSession> tradingSession()
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • builder

      public static final OrderCreate.Builder builder()

    • withAssetType

      public OrderCreate withAssetType(AssetType assetType)
      The type of the asset in this order, which must be one of the following: EQUITY, MUTUAL_FUND, and FIXED_INCOME.

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public OrderCreate withBrokerCapacity(BrokerCapacity brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • withBrokerCapacity

      public OrderCreate withBrokerCapacity(Optional<? extends BrokerCapacity> brokerCapacity)
      Defaults to "AGENCY" if not specified. For Equities: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Mutual Funds: Only "AGENCY" is allowed. For Fixed Income: Either "AGENCY" or "PRINCIPAL" are allowed.

    • withClientOrderId

      public OrderCreate withClientOrderId(String clientOrderId)
      User-supplied unique order ID. Cannot be more than 40 characters long.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OrderCreate withClientReceivedTime(OffsetDateTime clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • withClientReceivedTime

      public OrderCreate withClientReceivedTime(org.openapitools.jackson.nullable.JsonNullable<OffsetDateTime> clientReceivedTime)
      Required for Equity Orders for any client who is having Apex do CAT reporting on their behalf. A value may be provided for non-Equity orders, and will be remembered, but valid timestamps will have no impact on how they are processed.

    • withCommission

      public OrderCreate withCommission(CommissionCreate commission)
      A custom commission applied to an order

    • withCommission

      public OrderCreate withCommission(Optional<? extends CommissionCreate> commission)
      A custom commission applied to an order

    • withCurrencyCode

      public OrderCreate withCurrencyCode(String currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withCurrencyCode

      public OrderCreate withCurrencyCode(Optional<String> currencyCode)
      Defaults to "USD". Only "USD" is supported. Full list of currency codes is defined at: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217

    • withFees

      public OrderCreate withFees(List<FeeCreate> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • withFees

      public OrderCreate withFees(Optional<? extends List<FeeCreate>> fees)
      Fees that will be applied to this order. Only the BROKER_FEE type is supported.

    • withIdentifier

      public OrderCreate withIdentifier(String identifier)
      Identifier of the asset (of the type specified in `identifier_type`).

    • withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode

      public OrderCreate withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode(String identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode

      public OrderCreate withIdentifierIssuingRegionCode(Optional<String> identifierIssuingRegionCode)
      A string attribute denoting the country of issuance or where the asset is trading. Only available for Mutual Fund orders. Defaults to US, when trading non US mutual funds this field must be provided Complies with ISO-3166 Alpha-2 Codes

    • withIdentifierType

      public OrderCreate withIdentifierType(IdentifierType identifierType)
      The identifier type of the asset being ordered. For Equities: only SYMBOL is supported For Mutual Funds: only SYMBOL and CUSIP are supported For Fixed Income: only CUSIP and ISIN are supported

    • withLetterOfIntent

      public OrderCreate withLetterOfIntent(LetterOfIntentCreate letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

    • withLetterOfIntent

      public OrderCreate withLetterOfIntent(Optional<? extends LetterOfIntentCreate> letterOfIntent)
      Letter of Intent (LOI). An LOI allows investors to receive sales charge discounts based on a commitment to buy a specified monetary amount of shares over a period of time, usually 13 months.

      public OrderCreate withLimitPrice(LimitPriceCreate limitPrice)
      A limit price definition

      public OrderCreate withLimitPrice(Optional<? extends LimitPriceCreate> limitPrice)
      A limit price definition

    • withMaxSellQuantity

      public OrderCreate withMaxSellQuantity(DecimalCreate maxSellQuantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

      public OrderCreate withMaxSellQuantity(Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> maxSellQuantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      public OrderCreate withNotionalValue(DecimalCreate notionalValue)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

      public OrderCreate withNotionalValue(Optional<? extends DecimalCreate> notionalValue)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

    • withOrderDate

      public OrderCreate withOrderDate(DateCreate orderDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • withOrderType

      public OrderCreate withOrderType(OrderType orderType)
      The execution type of this order. For Equities: MARKET, and LIMIT are supported. For Mutual Funds: only MARKET is supported. For Fixed Income: only LIMIT is supported.

    • withQuantity

      public OrderCreate withQuantity(DecimalCreate quantity)
      A representation of a decimal value, such as 2.5. Clients may convert values into language-native decimal formats, such as Java's [BigDecimal][] or Python's [decimal.Decimal][].

      [BigDecimal]: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/math/BigDecimal.html [decimal.Decimal]: https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html

      public OrderCreate withRightsOfAccumulation(RightsOfAccumulationCreate rightsOfAccumulation)
      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

      Rights of Accumulation (ROA). An ROA allows an investor to aggregate their own fund shares with the holdings of certain related parties toward achieving the investment thresholds at which sales charge discounts become available.

      public OrderCreate withTimeInForce(TimeInForce timeInForce)
      Must be the value "DAY". Regulatory requirements dictate that the system capture the intended time_in_force, which is why this a mandatory field.

    • withTradingSession

      public OrderCreate withTradingSession(TradingSession tradingSession)
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.

    • withTradingSession

      public OrderCreate withTradingSession(Optional<? extends TradingSession> tradingSession)
      Which TradingSession to trade in, defaults to 'CORE'. Only available for Equity orders.
